steps.title: Use the doubling relationship
steps.content: Each side of triangle B is double the length of each side of triangle A. Start from the side length $320$ millimeters and multiply by $2$.

steps.title: Identify the given side length
steps.content: Triangle A has each side equal to 320 millimeters.

steps.title: Apply the doubling relationship
steps.content:
Triangle B's sides are double the length of triangle A's sides. Multiply:
$$2 \times 320 = 640 \text{ millimeters}$$
The question asks for one side length of triangle B, not perimeter or area. That confirms C is correct.
